channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 12 dead, dozens hurt as a bus overturns and passengers are thrown on a highway in South Africa
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — A bus overturned on a South African highway Tuesday and passengers were thrown out of it, killing at least 12 people and injuring 45 in Johannesburg, emergency services said.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE GUMEDE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Doctors give Pope Francis good news that he's no longer in imminent danger from pneumonia
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis woke up Tuesday to good news from his doctors: They upgraded his prognosis and say he is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of the double pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month in the longest and gravest threat to his 12-year papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Father of missing US student Sudiksha Konanki asks for wider investigation in Dominican Republic
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The father of a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki, who went missing in the popular Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana while on spring break with friends, said he has asked authorities to widen their investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Uganda deploys special forces to South Sudan to protect the government as fears of civil war grow
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Uganda has deployed an unknown number of troops to South Sudan in a bid to protect the fragile government of President Salva Kiir as a tense rivalry with his deputy threatens a return to civil war in the east African nation.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the Menendez brothers' bid for freedom
item.description: The district attorney of Los Angeles County says he does not support resentencing for Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about the Menendez brothers' bid for freedom
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles says he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez, brothers who have spent more than 30 years in prison for killing their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: InfoWars reporter Jamie White 'brutally murdered' near Austin residential area, outlet says
item.description: An InfoWars reporter was murdered in Austin, Texas, on Sunday night, according to Alex Jones. Austin Police Department is investigating the homicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Immigration needs to be 'shut down' until unity restored: Carlson
item.description: President Donald Trump has pledged history's largest domestic deportation, but some fear how long the process could take
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: 3 killed after medical helicopter crashes near Natchez Trace
item.description: MADISON COUNTY, Miss. (WJTV) - Three people have died after a medical helicopter crashed near the Natchez Trace in Madison County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, NewsNation

item.title: Florida spring break: Fort Lauderdale welcomes partiers as state sends more police to vacation hot spots
item.description: Fort Lauderdale and Miami Beach, Florida, are taking different approaches to spring break. One city is welcoming college students while the other is telling them to go somewhere else.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump DOT chief rejects inclusion of environmental justice factors in infrastructure decisions
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy on Monday rescinded Biden-era memos that he said called on state agencies to factor a “social justice and environmental agenda” into infrastructure project decisions.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One of Earth’s most ferocious predators may have looked very different than we thought
item.description: Few prehistoric monsters capture the imagination quite like the megalodon. From natural history museums to the silver screen, this colossal shark, which went extinct over three million years ago, has been depicted as one of the most terrifying and ferocious predators to ever roam the planet.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Stroller accessory recalled after 6 children suffer finger lacerations: CPSC
item.description: Consumers who may have purchased or installed the accessories are being instructed to immediately discontinue use.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man caught on camera using a lighter before garage bursts into flames
item.description: The video shows him flicking the lighter once more before walking out of frame. Later, another video shows the garage completely engulfed in flames.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, NewsNation

item.title: Wyoming billionaire 1 of 4 in race to buy TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump is considering offers from four groups to purchase TikTok, which must be sold by April 5.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Para climber reaches new heights after injury, wants others to join her
item.description: "The battle's not against my competitors, like the battle is about how well I do," said Nat Vorel, a paraclimber.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Ogle/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Man finds money in Tennessee park, hands it over to police
item.description: When asked why he didn't keep the cash, Ryan Soules said, "Well, not my money, simply, but I didn't earn it. Somebody else did.
item.dc:creator: Andy Cordan, NewsNation

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Missouri on the verge of taking over St. Louis police
item.description: The move comes nearly 13 years after voters returned police oversight to local leaders for the first time since the Civil War.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A single-engine plane crashes near a Pennsylvania airport and all 5 aboard are taken to hospitals
item.description: A single-engine airplane carrying five people crashed and burst into flames Sunday in the parking lot of a retirement community near a small airport in suburban Pennsylvania, and everyone on board survived, officials and witnesses said. The fiery crash happened around 3 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Firefighters knock down wind-driven brush fire on Long Island but risk remains with high winds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Firefighters in New York knocked down a dwindling brush fire in a wooded stretch of Long Island on Sunday as officials warned that high wind gusts could leave the region vulnerable to additional blazes. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges against Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy
item.description: Xavier Worthy was arrested Friday and has since been released after the county's district attorney said he would not accept the case at this time.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Hateful message left on restaurant receipt results in outpouring of support, staff says
item.description: The public is rallying behind a central Ohio Mexican restaurant after a customer left a hateful message instead of a tip.
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, Fox 5

item.title: Couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, WFLA, NBC

item.title: CDC launches 'conflicts of interest' page about vaccine advisory panel
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) published a page on Friday listing the conflicts of interest reported by members of a key vaccine advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Authorities in Alaska said they would attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers, who were believed to have died after being buried in snow.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4
